use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

struct Connection {
    id: u64,
    created_at: Instant,
}

struct ConnectionPool {
    connections: Mutex<Vec<Connection>>,
    counter: std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64,
    max_size: usize,
    max_idle: Duration,
    max_lifetime: Duration,
}

impl ConnectionPool {
    // GOOD: bounded pool with max size, idle timeout, and connection lifetime
    fn new(max_size: usize, max_idle: Duration, max_lifetime: Duration) -> Self {
        Self {
            connections: Mutex::new(Vec::with_capacity(max_size)),
            counter: std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64::new(0),
            max_size,
            max_idle,
            max_lifetime,
        }
    }

    fn acquire(&self) -> Option<Connection> {
        let mut pool = self.connections.lock().unwrap();
        let now = Instant::now();

        while let Some(conn) = pool.pop() {
            if now.duration_since(conn.created_at) < self.max_lifetime {
                return Some(conn);
            }
        }

        if self.counter.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed) < self.max_size as u64 {
            self.counter.fetch_add(1, std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed);
            return Some(Connection {
                id: self.counter.load(std::sync::atomic::Ordering::Relaxed),
                created_at: now,
            });
        }

        None
    }

    fn release(&self, conn: Connection) {
        let mut pool = self.connections.lock().unwrap();
        if pool.len() < self.max_size && conn.created_at.elapsed() < self.max_idle {
            pool.push(conn);
        }
    }
}
